description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a wooden shingled roof supported by sturdy timber beams. The well features a visible wooden bucket attached to a rope and pulley mechanism, which is mounted on a horizontal wooden axle between the two main support posts. The stonework is irregular and weathered, giving the well an aged, rustic appearance, while the roof is made of overlapping, mossy wooden shingles. The protective structure consists of a low stone wall encircling the well opening, with the roof providing shelter from above. The wooden framework is robust, with cross-bracing for stability, and the bucket is suspended over the well opening, ready for use. No decorative elements are present, and the design is purely functional.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden shingled roof, timber supports, and a bucket-and-pulley mechanism. The well is sturdy, weathered, and functional, with no decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular stone well, timber supports, and the shingled roof, with the bucket not visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden cross-bracing, pulley mechanism, and the depth of the roof structure.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with the wooden bucket clearly visible hanging over the well opening.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the bucket and pulley mechanism from a different angle, with the stone wall and timber structure prominent.
attribute_well_cover_design: No solid cover; open well with a wooden shingled roof overhead for protection.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden bucket is suspended by a rope and pulley mechanism mounted on a horizontal wooden axle between the support posts.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high surrounds the well opening, with a wooden shingled roof supported by timber beams.
